flow: IPatchPlanner proposes a PatchPlan -> AssessRisk -> apply ONLY in an isolated worktree via IsolatedPatchRunner (build/test gated) -> rollback on failure -> record experience -> emit KnowledgeProposal(Approved=false)
riskRules: every target path must be inside the worktree sandbox
forbidden command in a .ps1 patch is blocked

scenarios.name: safe change
scenarios.outcome: accepted (build + tests green); knowledge proposal emitted

honest: Tested with a FAKE planner, not a live model. A real local model would only PROPOSE plans, which are then risk-assessed, sandboxed, build/test-gated, and rolled back on failure. A human still approves before anything reaches the main repo. This is a safe validated core, not an unattended autonomous agent.
